channel.title: County Fights on Detention Pipeline
channel.description: Recent content in County Fights on Detention Pipeline

item.title: Palm Beach County FL — 'Belly of the Beast' Immigration Enforcement and Community Resistance
item.description: Palm Beach County leads all of Florida in immigration arrests — described as “the belly of the beast” by the Florida Immigrant Coalition. The county’s enforcement apparatus includes:

item.title: Pinal County AZ — County Attorney Signs Rogue 287(g), Board Declares It Illegal
item.description: In December 2025, Pinal County Attorney Brad Miller unilaterally signed a 287(g) agreement with ICE that went beyond the existing jail-based program, effectively turning his office’s 10 investigators…

item.title: Durant OK — Choctaw Nation Purchases Warehouse to Block ICE Detention Facility
item.description: In one of the most creative and effective resistance actions against the ICE warehouse expansion, the Choctaw Nation purchased a warehouse in Durant, Oklahoma that DHS had targeted for ICE detention…

item.title: Bradford County FL — 3,000-Bed IGSA Facility on Contaminated Site
item.description: Bradford County is the documented case study of the county-led IGSA model — where a consulting firm ([[sabot-consulting|Sabot Consulting]]) staffed by former ICE officials pitches rural sheriffs on…

item.title: Hanover VA — Warehouse Owners Terminated Deal with ICE
item.description: Warehouse owners in Hanover County, Virginia terminated their deal with ICE. Along with [[shakopee-mn-rejected]], [[kansas-city-mo-platform-ventures]], and Chester NY, this represents the pattern of…

item.title: Kansas City MO — Platform Ventures Warehouse Deal Blocked by Community Pressure
item.description: Platform Ventures, the developer that owned an $80M warehouse in south Kansas City, backed out of selling it to ICE on February 12, 2026 after weeks of sustained community pressure. The warehouse had…

item.title: Merrimack NH — ICE Processing Center Paused After Community Opposition
item.description: The Merrimack fight produced the single most important disclosure in the detention expansion story: the New Hampshire state government released internal ICE planning documents that explained the…

item.title: Romulus MI — State AG and City Sue to Block ICE Warehouse Near Schools
item.description: ICE purchased a warehouse at 7525 Cogswell Street in Romulus, Michigan (see [[romulus-mi-warehouse]]) in February 2026 without notifying the state, city, or public. Michigan AG Dana Nessel and the…

item.title: Roxbury NJ — Governor, AG, and Township Sue to Block ICE Processing Center
item.description: ICE purchased a 470,000 sq ft warehouse in Roxbury Township, Morris County (see [[roxbury-nj-warehouse]]). The Governor, Attorney General, and Township all filed suit to block the conversion….

item.title: Salt Lake City UT — Protests, Water Restrictions, and Mayor Opposition to 10,000-Bed Mega Center
item.description: ICE purchased an 833,000 sq ft warehouse (see [[salt-lake-city-ut-mega-center]]) for $145.4 million through a Delaware shell company on March 12, 2026. Mayor Erin Mendenhall disclosed that ICE plans…

item.title: Shakopee MN — Warehouse Owner Declined ICE Deal
item.description: The warehouse owner in Shakopee, Minnesota declined to sell to ICE after community opposition made the deal politically toxic. This follows the [[kansas-city-mo-platform-ventures|Kansas City model]]:…

item.title: Social Circle GA — City Locked the Water Meter to Block 10,000-Bed Mega Center
item.description: The city of Social Circle, Georgia locked the water meter at the site of a planned 7,500-10,000 bed ICE mega center (see [[social-circle-ga-mega-center]]) — because the facility’s projected sewage…

item.title: Surprise AZ — City Passed 5-Year Ban on Detention Facilities (Unenforceable Due to Federal Preemption)
item.description: Surprise, AZ passed a five-year ban on detention facilities after learning DHS had purchased a $70M+ warehouse without notifying the city council. The ban is unenforceable — federal preemption means…
